summary: Soundboard recording with clear detail highlights a chilling “Days Between” and an energized “Not Fade Away”, while Jerry’s slower vocals on “So Many Roads” add a bittersweet edge.
review: The Miller SBD capture is generally good, delivering crisp instrument separation but a heavy‑metal‑leaning mix for Weir that some find jarring. Musically the show shines with an out‑of‑space “Days Between” and a surprisingly lively “Not Fade Away”, yet Jerry’s worn vocal on “So Many Roads” tempers the overall excitement.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is mixed (standout jams vs. slower vocals) while recording quality is good; combined weighted score yields ~3.2.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear soundboard source; crisp separation but the mix emphasizes heavy‑metal tones on Weir, creating a tonal contrast with Garcia’s softer acoustic parts.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set leans heavy on Weir, creating a jarring shift; second set closes strong with an energized “Not Fade Away” and encores, though the overall pacing feels uneven due to slower vocal delivery.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Voice sounds worn and slower, adding melancholy to “So Many Roads” and “Days Between”, but his slide work still holds up.
